config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena sejarah sering menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi masyarakat” bisa dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat perlindungan hak dasar setiap orang, bukan dengan menekan minoritas atau membungkam suara yang berbeda. Kalau hak individu kuat, masyarakat juga justru lebih adil dan sehat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power. Public health, safety, and basic order do matter, but they should justify limits on individual rights only when those limits are narrow, necessary, and accountable. I also disagree with the idea that society and individual rights are opposites, since a society that routinely crushes personal liberty usually ends up less free and less trustworthy for everyone. The burden should be on society to prove why a right must be limited, not on individuals to prove why they deserve it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, mulai dari pembungkaman suara kritis sampai perlakuan tidak adil terhadap kelompok kecil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru akan lebih sehat kalau dibangun di atas perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dengan mengorbankan orang per orang. Saya setuju bahwa ada situasi tertentu seperti keselamatan publik atau krisis besar, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, jelas, dan tidak boleh jadi alasan untuk menekan kebebasan terus-menerus.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of language has too often been used to excuse overreach by governments and institutions. Yes, there are real cases where public safety or a crisis justifies limited restrictions, but those limits need to be narrow, temporary, and easy to challenge. What matters most to me is that individual rights are the default, not a privilege society can suspend whenever it claims a collective benefit. A society is strongest when it protects people from the majority, not when it gives the majority a blank check.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutup kritik dan menekan kelompok yang kecil atau tidak populer. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tapi menurut saya justru harus dibangun dari perlindungan hak dasar setiap orang, bukan dengan menjadikan individu sebagai korban demi mayoritas. Saya bisa menerima pembatasan yang sangat terbatas saat darurat atau demi keselamatan publik, tetapi harus jelas, proporsional, dan mudah diawasi supaya tidak berubah jadi pembenaran penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From an American perspective, history makes me wary of any argument that gives the majority or the state broad permission to override personal liberty, because that often turns into censorship, discrimination, or abuse of power. Society does have legitimate needs, especially in emergencies or for public safety, but those limits should be narrow, temporary, and accountable rather than treated as the default. To me, a healthy society is one that protects individual rights first and only restricts them when it can clearly justify why that is necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
